name: mock_font_exact_metrics
description: Text metrics must be EXACT, not 'roughly right'. `Azul Mock Mono` is a mock font committed to the repo (assets/fonts/test/, generated by scripts/gen_mock_fonts.py): every glyph advances exactly 0.5em, ascent 0.8em, descent 0.2em. It is registered as an ordinary in-memory font in the shared FcFontCache (FontManager::register_named_font), so a stylesheet naming it travels the SAME path a system font takes -- CSS font-family -> font-stack collection -> chain resolution -> FontId -> load_missing_for_chains -> shaping -- with no test-only bypass. At font-size 20px it is exactly 10px per glyph and a 20px line box. This scenario asserts that arithmetic end-to-end through a mount: (1) a single line of text is exactly 20px tall (a system fallback measured 28.96px here, which is exactly how nobody noticed that font-family was being ignored); (2) 'HELLO HELLO' (2 words x 50px + a 10px space = 110px) in a 60px-wide box wraps into EXACTLY 2 lines = 40px, which is only true if each glyph advances exactly 10px; (3) the same string in `Azul Mock Wide` (1em advance = 20px/glyph) is exactly 20px per line and each 100px word needs its own line. Two families, two different measurements, is the proof that font-family actually selects the font instead of collapsing onto one fallback FontId -- the bug this test was written for.

css: #one  { width: 60px; font-family: "Azul Mock Mono"; font-size: 20px; color: black; }
#two  { width: 60px; font-family: "Azul Mock Mono"; font-size: 20px; color: black; }
#wide { width: 60px; font-family: "Azul Mock Wide"; font-size: 20px; color: black; }
